How it works

From measurements to money in three steps.

1

Scan the plan with AI

Snap a photo of the window schedule and the AI reads every opening — counts, sizes, and types — in seconds. Prefer to type it? Enter the linear footage by hand.

2

Get instant pricing & approvals

Materials, labor, and your markup — calculated to the foot. Plus the matching Florida product approval and its screw pattern, no digging required.

3

Send the quote, hand off the cut list

The homeowner gets a clean, branded quote to sign. Your crew gets the buck cut list and materials list — the same job, every document.

Every job, fully documented

Enter the job once. Get every document.

Anchor generates everything the customer, office, and crew need — the moment your numbers are in.

For the field

Materials List

Turns the job into a shopping list — exact pack quantities, ready to check off in the field. No guessing how many cases, rolls, or boards you need.

  • ✓ Raw quantities converted to cases, rolls & boards
  • ✓ Check-off format built for the supply run
  • ✓ Recalculates the instant the job changes

Buck Cut List

Packs every opening's cuts onto the fewest boards possible — exact lengths, minimal offcut. The cut list that takes a foreman 20 minutes on a notepad, done instantly.

  • ✓ Bin-packing optimizer — fewest boards, least waste
  • ✓ Exact board lengths and cut order
  • ✓ Built from the openings you scan or enter

Why installers switch to Anchor

Built for the people doing the install.

Stop guessing on labor and materials.

Anchor prices every job by the linear foot, broken down by frame, glass, install, and overhead — the same way the crew thinks about it. No more "I forgot the shim packs" surprises eating your margin.

Florida product approvals at your fingertips.

Type the FL# off the sticker. Get the screw pattern, substrate-by-substrate, in two seconds — not twenty minutes of floridabuilding.org PDFs.

AI reads your plan.

Drop in a sheet from the architect. Anchor pulls every opening, sizes them up, and pre-fills your job — minutes saved on every takeoff.

Your customer sees a clean, branded quote in 60 seconds.

Send a PDF or a shareable link. Your logo, your colors, your terms — not a spreadsheet screenshot. Wins more bids; gets you paid faster.

FAQ

Questions, answered.

Can I switch plans later?
Yes — upgrade or downgrade anytime from your account; we prorate the difference.
What happens at the end of my free trial?
Your account stays active in read-only mode until you pick a plan. Your saved jobs and brand profile stay put.
What counts as a "quote"?
Each time you save a new job (not a draft) it counts as a quote for your monthly limit. Editing an already-saved job is free.
Do you charge for the AI plan-reader separately?
AI plan reads are included on every paid plan — 2 a month on Starter, 100 a month on Pro, and unlimited on Shop — at no extra charge. The free trial includes a few so you can try it.
Is there a setup fee?
No. Sign up, pick a plan, start quoting.
Can I get a refund?
Yes — within 14 days of your first paid charge, no questions asked.
Is there a contract?
No. Cancel anytime; you keep access through the end of your billing cycle.

Quote your next job in minutes.

No logo

Step 1 of 5

Welcome — here's how it works

Building a quote takes about 30 seconds.

  1. 1
    Pick your job specs — manufacturer, construction type, application, stories.
  2. 2
    Enter the total linear footage — or Scan a Plan with AI to fill it in automatically.
  3. 3
    Read your instant price — with a full cost breakdown and materials list.
  4. 4
    Download what you need — a customer quote, materials list, or buck cut list.

About your numbers

The calculator is pre-loaded with sample rates so you can try it right now. But your real costs — labor, caulking, screws, shims — vary company to company.

Update them as you go. Quotes get sharper over time — there's no rush, and you can change anything anytime in Settings.

Print it and track your pricing in the field
A blank checklist of every material — keep it on hand and fill in pricing as you source each item (they often come from different places).

Your core rates

These two drive most of every quote. Set them if you know them — or leave them and update later.

$ / hr · per installer

What you pay each crew member hourly. Crew size lives in Settings → Labor Model.

%

Your window manufacturers

Add the brands you install. Keep, edit, or remove the pre-loaded ones, and add your own.

Each brand has two screw patterns per LF — nail-fin and through-frame. Don't have exact counts yet? Leave the defaults and fine-tune later.

You're all set

You're ready to build quotes. The more you dial in your real costs, the more accurate every quote — update anytime in Settings.

Items still on demo numbers show an amber Unverified tag until you confirm them.

The Job

Everything's pre-set for a typical Florida job — change only what's different.

Is this a new build, or replacing windows in an existing home?

Concrete-block walls, or wood-frame walls?

How many floors — upper floors add labor (and sometimes a lift).

Add up the width of every window opening, in feet.

ft

How the window attaches to the wall — match what the job uses.

Hurricane-rated (impact) glass costs more than standard glass.

Upload Window Schedule

AI reads it and fills the window count, LF, manufacturer, and buck cut list

⚡ Upload your plan — we read it for you
PDF, PNG, or JPEG of the window schedule. One upload fills the window count, total LF, and manufacturer — and builds your buck cut list — automatically. No Grok tab, no copy/paste.

AI reads the schedule — the calculator optimizes the board cuts

⚡ Upload your plan — we read it for you
PDF, PNG, or JPEG of the window/door schedule. One upload builds the cut list — and fills the window count, total LF, and manufacturer — automatically. No Grok tab, no copy/paste.

    Settings

    Saved
    Brand & Company Logo, company name & contact info
    Appears on customer quotes and the materials list.
    No logo
    Company Logo
    Job Defaults Default brand, application & door toggles
    Your typical setup. Clicking New Job returns to these values.
    Window Manufacturers Per-brand screw patterns & impact spec
    Each brand's screw pattern, per linear foot. Nail-fin screws are used on nail-fin installs; through-frame anchors on Equal/Unequal Leg installs.

    Through-frame values are placeholder estimates — verify per brand.

    Customers
    Tap a customer to open their page — edit their info or browse their jobs. Changes apply to all of that customer's saved jobs.
    Rates & Pricing
    Margins, taxes, labor, and what every material costs.
    Money & Tax Markup, materials tax & impact labor
    The numbers that drive every quote — change these first.
    Added on top of your cost to get the selling price.
    Sales tax on materials — auto-set from your county on the Brand tab.
    Extra labor multiplier for hurricane-impact jobs.
    Labor Install speed, crew size & pay
    Crew & Time
    Internal
    Labor cost = crew-hours × what the crew costs/hr — marked up by your job margin like materials.
    How many people on the crew.
    What you pay each crew member per hour.
    Per opening: tools, measure, level, anchor, cleanup.
    Per job: load the truck, drive, stage on arrival.
    Crew costs you —

    Window Rates

    Swing Doors Single / double leaf, sill flashing

    Priced per door, not per LF. Threshold hardware is included in the per-door materials cost. Tune below — defaults are construction-shop estimates, verify against your suppliers.

    Single-leaf
    Labor min / door
    Materials $ / door (incl. threshold)
    Anchors each / door
    Double-leaf
    Labor min / door
    Materials $ / door (incl. threshold)
    Anchors each / door
    Sill flashing

    Priced per LF of total door width. Toggle on/off per job in the rail. Door widths set the flashing length each door type contributes.

    Rate $ / LF
    Single width LF
    Double width LF
    Sliding Glass Doors Labor, panel size & screw inspection

    Priced separately from windows. Labor scales by panel count. Frame footage still pulls per-LF screws, caulk, shims & bucking automatically — only the slider-specific extras live here. Defaults are construction-shop estimates; verify against your crew & suppliers.

    Labor
    Crew-hours / panel hr / panel

    A 3-panel door = 3× this. Heavy-panel install + snap covers.

    Slider-only materials
    Sill / track flashing $ / LF width
    Extra anchors each / panel
    Default panel size

    Used as a fallback when a door has no overall size: width = panel width × panels, height = this height.

    Panel width in
    Height in
    Screw inspection (extra day)

    Sliders need a building-dept screw inspection before panels & snap covers go on — almost always a return trip on another day.

    Bifold Doors Labor, panel size & screw inspection

    Bifold / folding glass walls, priced separately from windows. The most labor-intensive opening — labor scales by folding leaf. Frame footage pulls per-LF screws, caulk, shims & bucking automatically but is never added to your window LF; only the bifold-specific extras live here. Defaults are construction-shop estimates; verify against your crew & suppliers.

    Labor
    Crew-hours / panel hr / panel

    A 4-leaf door = 4× this. Each folding leaf is hinged, leveled & aligned.

    Bifold-only materials
    Sill / track flashing $ / LF width
    Extra anchors each / panel
    Default panel size

    Used as a fallback when a door has no overall size: width = panel width × panels, height = this height.

    Panel width in
    Height in
    Screw inspection (extra day)

    Bifolds need a building-dept screw inspection before panels & snap covers go on — almost always a return trip on another day.

    Florida Product Approvals

    Screw patterns by FL# — match the number on the unit's NFRC sticker.

    Your saved approvals
    Reference only — always verify the exact FL# on the unit's NFRC label and download the latest installation PDF from floridabuilding.org before use. Patterns must match the installed product's size, DP rating, and substrate.
